name: Alex Tan
summary: Software developer with 2+ years building and shipping web applications end to end. Comfortable across the stack — React on the frontend, Node.js/Express on the backend, PostgreSQL for storage — with a focus on shipping small, working pieces fast and iterating from there.

bullets: Built and shipped a customer-facing booking dashboard in React and TypeScript, cutting average support-ticket resolution time by 30% by surfacing order history directly in the UI.
Designed and implemented 12 REST API endpoints in Node.js/Express backing the dashboard, with input validation and consistent error responses across all routes.
Migrated a legacy MySQL schema to PostgreSQL with zero downtime, writing the migration scripts and rollback plan myself.
Set up a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that runs the test suite and lints on every pull request, catching an average of 4-5 issues per week before merge.
Paired with two junior interns on their first feature tickets, reviewing PRs and walking through debugging approaches rather than just fixing issues for them.
Maintained and extended a Vue.js inventory management app used daily by ~40 internal staff, fixing bugs and adding small features from a shared backlog.
Wrote unit tests with Jest for a previously untested billing module, raising coverage from 12% to 68% over four months.
Integrated a third-party payment API (Stripe), handling webhook events for payment confirmation and refunds.
Documented internal API endpoints in Swagger/OpenAPI so new hires could onboard without asking a senior dev to walk them through it.

projects.name: Habit Tracker Web App
bullets: Built a small habit-tracking dashboard in vanilla JavaScript and CSS with a GitHub-style contribution heatmap, persisting data to a local JSON file.

projects.name: Repo Health CLI
bullets: Wrote a Node.js CLI tool that scans a git repo for commit frequency, stale branches, and accidentally committed secrets, using the GitHub API.
